text: ‘It is four whole months’; this may have been a proverb, meaning that there was no hurry, like our ‘Rome was not built in a day’. Our Lord here rejects it (whereas he approves of another proverb in verse 37 below); it cannot be too soon to begin preaching his gospel.

The bearing of this verse, which has been the subject of much dispute among commentators, is most simply explained if we understand it as meaning that our Lord had notyet won favour in his own country of Galilee (Cf. 2.23; 4.41).
